source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the final of a dancing competition, each of the three members of the jury gives each of the five competitors 0 points, 1 point, 2 points, 3 points or 4 points. No two competitors get the same mark from any individual judge. Adam knows all the sums of the marks and a few single marks, as shown. How many points does Adam get from judge III? <image1>

image_dependency: Question says 'as shown', indicating essential information is in the image
The task likely depends on reading a score table/grid of sums and given entries that is not fully specified in text alone
Image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual source is available
multi_step: The puzzle combines multiple constraints: 3 judges, 5 competitors, marks 0-4, and no repeated mark per judge
Solving Adam's mark from judge III requires integrating row/column totals with individual known cells
This is not a direct lookup; it requires elimination/deduction across several entities
Numeric puzzle structure suggests at least two or more reasoning steps rather than single-step recognition
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('1')
Constraint-based puzzle solutions can be checked against table sums and uniqueness rules
Asset registry confirms question and answer are present and registry_passed=true
A proposed solution can be validated deterministically from the visible grid constraints
